generated_oml:
// C2: Physical Acting Components
instance OccupancyActuator : DTDFVocab:ActuatingComponent [
    base:desc "Building control actuator for occupancy-based lighting/HVAC adjustments in LEED Platinum academic building"
]

instance HVACController : DTDFVocab:ActuatingComponent [
    base:desc "Smart HVAC controller for energy optimization in BIM-based DT infrastructure"
]

instance SmartLightingSystem : DTDFVocab:ActuatingComponent [
    base:desc "Modular smart lighting system with adaptive control logic for building occupancy management"
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancyDetector : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-enabled occupancy detector embedded in building infrastructure providing real-time occupancy status"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmission
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT temperature sensor measuring environmental conditions in building infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmission
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT humidity sensor tracking moisture levels in building air quality monitoring system"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmission
]

instance PostOccupancyFeedbackSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy data feed capturing user satisfaction metrics and behavior patterns"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PostOccupancyFeedbackDataTransmission
]

// C4: Data Transmission Components
instance OccupancyDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancyDetector
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance PostOccupancyFeedbackDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PostOccupancyFeedbackSensor
]

// C6: Data Models
instance BIMEnrichedModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enriched BIM model containing device configurations, control logic, and IoT sensor metadata for DT deployment"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMTooling
]

instance RuntimeInterfaceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model generated from BIM enrichment containing runtime interfaces for Eclipse Hono/Ditto middleware"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MiddlewareConfigurator
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "BIM-based control logic model defining adaptive policies for lighting/HVAC systems"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ReinforcementLearningEngine
]

// C7: Data Types
instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time occupancy status data streamed from IoT sensors for building automation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmission
]

instance EnvironmentalMetricsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Combination of temperature and humidity data for comprehensive building environmental monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmission, HumidityDataTransmission
]

instance PostOccupancyFeedback : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "User behavior and satisfaction data collected post-occupancy for continuous improvement"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PostOccupancyFeedbackDataTransmission
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono middleware providing real-time IoT connectivity for bidirectional data exchange between physical assets and DT"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, SimulationAndOptimizationService
]

instance Ditto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Standardized interoperable data storage and synchronization platform for DT components"
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomServiceIntegration
]

instance UnrealEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Game engine platform for immersive 3D visualization of building states and operational metrics"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance BIMTooling : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "BIM software tools (Revit/ArchiCAD) used for device modeling and control logic definition"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMEnrichedModel
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineering : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-driven engineering framework automating BIM semantics extraction and runtime artifact generation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RuntimeInterfaceModel
]

instance MiddlewareConfigurator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Configurator tool for extracting metadata and generating runtime interfaces for Eclipse Hono/Ditto"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RuntimeInterfaceModel
]

instance ReinforcementLearningEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "BOREALIS reinforcement learning framework for iterative control policy optimization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicModel
]

// C10: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization platform using Unreal Engine for real-time building state monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides OccupancyVisualization, EnergyConsumptionVisualization
]

instance SimulationAndOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "End-to-end automation pipeline for life-cycle-aware building optimization via BIM-based control logic"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationRecommendations, CostReductionAnalysis
]

instance CustomServiceIntegration : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Modular extension framework allowing addition of analytics/plugins via standardized extension points"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceAnalytics, AdaptiveLightingControl
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OccupancyVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D visualization of building occupancy patterns in real-time via Unreal Engine"
]

instance EnergyConsumptionVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive display of energy consumption metrics across building systems"
]

instance EnergyOptimizationRecommendations : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics recommendations for adaptive lighting/HVAC systems based on real-time data"
]

instance CostReductionAnalysis : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Financial impact analysis of energy optimization strategies and operational improvements"
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAnalytics : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Analytics service predicting maintenance needs based on sensor data patterns"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AdaptiveLightingControl : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Reinforcement learning-based control strategy for dynamic lighting adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PolicyUpdateManagement : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of control policies through reinforcement learning iterations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DynamicConfigurationService : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Runtime configuration management enabling modular service extension without redeployment"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ClashDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "BIM-based detection of design/operational conflicts in building infrastructure deployment"
]

instance OverallDigitalTwinEntanglement : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative metric evaluating fidelity between physical and virtual building twins"
]

// C12: Environment Components
instance BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains OccupancyDetector, TemperatureSensor, HVACController, SmartLightingSystem
]

instance BIMBasedControlEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BIMEnrichedModel, ControlLogicModel, RuntimeInterfaceModel
]

instance RealTimeDataEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains OccupancyData, EnvironmentalMetricsData, PostOccupancyFeedback
]

instance MiddlewareIntegrationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains EclipseHono, Ditto, ModelDrivenEngineering
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** occurs through **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto**, where the DT generates control signals (e.g., actuator commands, lighting schedules) and transmits them back to the physical building systems. This bidirectional communication enables **closed-loop control** (e.g., adjusting HVAC based on real-time occupancy data) and supports **runtime adaptability** via reinforcement learning (e.g., BOREALIS project). The framework ensures **low-latency synchronization** to maintain fidelity between the DT and PT."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The **twinning time scale** is **real-time**, with synchronization rates optimized for **low-latency data exchange** between physical and virtual components. The framework leverages **Eclipse Hono/Ditto** for **sub-second latency** in data transmission, ensuring the DT reflects the PT’s operational state dynamically. The **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric** evaluates the fidelity of this synchronization, validating interoperability across the DT’s lifecycle phases (design, construction, operation)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is a **centralized, service-oriented architecture** with:
  - A **core DT framework** (hosted on Eclipse Hono/Ditto) that orchestrates bidirectional data flow between physical assets and the virtual model.
  - **Modular service integration**: Standardized extension points allow **custom services** (e.g., analytics, control plugins) to be added without redesigning the core.
  - **BIM-centric orchestration**: The DT is **tightly coupled** with BIM models, ensuring semantic alignment across lifecycle stages (design, construction, operation).
  - **Decentralized sub-twins**: Internal twins (e.g., lighting, HVAC) are managed as modular units, each with its own control logic (e.g., TwinLight’s layered architecture).
  - **Real-time synchronization**: The constellation ensures **low-latency updates** via Ditto/Eclipse Hono, maintaining the DT’s fidelity to the physical twin."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans **three lifecycle stages**:
  1. **Design phase**: Enriched BIM models incorporate **device configurations** and **control logic** (e.g., lighting schedules) via model-driven transformations.
  2. **Construction phase**: The DT is **automatically deployed** from BIM models, with real-time interfaces generated for IoT devices (e.g., sensors, actuators).
  3. **Operation phase**: The DT provides **real-time monitoring**, **predictive analytics**, and **adaptive control** (e.g., energy optimization via reinforcement learning, as in BOREALIS). The framework ensures **compatibility** with existing BIM standards (e.g., IFC) and supports **post-occupancy adjustments** (e.g., sensor data-driven policy updates)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model-driven transformations**: Automated extraction of BIM semantics reduces manual errors and ensures consistency across lifecycle stages.
  - **Real-time synchronization**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto enables **sub-second latency** in data exchange, minimizing drift between the DT and PT.
  - **ODTE metric**: Quantifies the **interoperability and architectural soundness** of the DT, validating its alignment with the physical twin.
  - **Verification/validation**: The framework includes **runtime validation** of control logic and sensor data, addressing **semantic drift** (e.g., via BOREALIS’ reinforcement learning).
  - **Uncertainty management**: While not explicitly quantified in the documents, the framework assumes **high-fidelity** for critical operations (e.g., energy optimization) and supports **adaptive adjustments** to account for operational variability."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **DT technical connection** relies on:
  - **Network protocols**: Eclipse Hono and Ditto use **standardized IoT protocols** (e.g., MQTT, CoAP) for real-time data exchange.
  - **Middleware architecture**: A **layered system** couples BIM with a **middleware layer** (Hono/Ditto) and a **visualization layer** (Unreal Engine).
  - **Bidirectional communication**: Data flows from physical sensors to the DT (via Ditto) and from the DT to actuators (via Hono), ensuring **closed-loop control**.
  - **Latency optimization**: The framework targets **sub-second synchronization** to maintain DT fidelity, with no explicit latency thresholds specified beyond real-time requirements."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is **hosted on a scalable, service-oriented platform** with:
  - **Centralized deployment**: The core DT framework runs on a **dedicated server** (not specified in documents) that orchestrates Hono/Ditto middleware.
  - **Modular extension points**: Services (e.g., analytics, visualization) are deployed as **microservices**, enabling horizontal scaling.
  - **Unreal Engine integration**: Visualization components are deployed as **interactive 3D applications**, likely hosted on cloud/edge infrastructure.
  - **BIM compatibility**: The DT is **BIM-agnostic** in deployment, leveraging existing BIM tools (e.g., Revit) for model input."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration occurs via:
  - **BIM interoperability**: The DT is designed to **exchange data with external systems** (e.g., facility management tools, IoT platforms) using **standardized BIM formats** (e.g., IFC).
  - **Middleware bridges**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto enable **cross-platform communication** with other IoT/OT systems (e.g., PLCs, smart meters).
  - **Standardized APIs**: The framework supports **modular service integration**, allowing DTs to interact with external analytics or control systems (e.g., via REST/gRPC).
  - **No explicit external DTs**: The documents focus on **internal DT orchestration**, but the modular architecture enables future integration with other DTs via standardized protocols."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership and privacy are addressed as follows:
  - **Physical twin (PT) ownership**: The **BIM model and operational data** are primarily owned by the building owner/facility manager, as the DT is derived from and synchronized with their assets.
  - **DT service provider**: Custom services (e.g., analytics plugins) may be developed by third-party DT Engineers, but the core DT infrastructure (Hono/Ditto) is likely **open-source** and non-proprietary.
  - **Privacy compliance**: The framework assumes adherence to **AECO data governance standards** (e.g., GDPR for operational data), though explicit privacy measures (e.g., encryption, access controls) are not detailed.
  - **No explicit anonymization**: The documents do not specify data anonymization techniques, but the DT’s modular design supports **scalable privacy controls** (e.g., selective data sharing)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to:
  - **BIM standards**: Uses **IFC (Industry Foundation Classes)** for BIM model exchange, ensuring interoperability with AECO tools (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD).
  - **IoT/OT protocols**: Leverages **Eclipse Hono** (MQTT/CoAP) and **Ditto** for standardized IoT connectivity.
  - **Model-driven engineering (MDE)**: Follows **MDE best practices** (e.g., model-to-model transformations) to automate DT deployment and reduce manual effort.
  - **Service-oriented architecture**: Supports **standardized extension points** for modular service integration, enabling compliance with **open-source DT frameworks** (e.g., Eclipse Ditto).
  - **No proprietary standards**: The documents emphasize **open-source tools** (Hono, Ditto, Unreal Engine) and **BIM-centric approaches**, avoiding vendor lock-in."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
  - **Network security**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto use **encrypted communication** (e.g., TLS) for data exchange between physical sensors and the DT.
  - **Runtime validation**: The framework includes **automated validation** of control logic and sensor data to prevent malfunctions (e.g., unsafe actuator commands).
  - **Remote operation safety**: The DT’s modular architecture allows **remote control** of physical systems (e.g., lighting/HVAC) but assumes **human-in-the-loop oversight** for critical operations (e.g., emergency systems).
  - **No explicit cybersecurity frameworks**: The documents do not detail **specific security protocols** (e.g., zero-trust architectures), but the use of standardized middleware (Hono/Ditto) supports **industry-standard security practices**.
  - **Human safety**: The DT is designed for **non-invasive monitoring** (e.g., occupancy tracking) and **predictive maintenance**, reducing risks associated with physical interventions."
]
